channel.title: Marriage-Bond on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in Marriage-Bond on Berrytree.org

item.title: Chapter 5: John Berry 1762–1804
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 4: Robert Berry Jr. | Table of Contents | Chapter 6: Joshua Berry →

Ancestor Profile: John Berry — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 5#

John Berry 1762 to 1804#

John Berry was probably the third child and second son born to Robert OC and Elizabeth Cate Berry. John was born in Orange County, North Carolina. Robert OC and Elizabeth apparently used the English naming order, which would make John Berry their third son. The first son would have been named Robert Jr. for his father or paternal grandfather. The second son would have been named John for his maternal grandfather. Elizabeth Cate’s father was John Cate, and her mother was Margery Lawrence Cate. John Berry was most likely born about 1762 and would have been a grown man by 1782. We know that John was married and had children because Robert Berry Sr. left a slave to the children of John Berry, deceased, in his 1812 will.

item.title: Chapter 6: Joshua Berry 1766–1838
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 5: John Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 7: Isaac Berry →

Ancestor Profile: Joshua Berry Sr. — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 6#

Joshua Berry 1766–1838#

Joshua Berry was probably the fourth child of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry. He was named for his mother’s oldest brother, Joshua Cate. I think Joshua was born around 1766, and I know his wife, Nancy Ellison, was born in 1775 based on census information taken from the 1850 Orange County, North Carolina, census. Joshua Berry, with his family and most of his descendants, is one of the four brothers whose families remained in Orange County, North Carolina.

item.title: Chapter 9: Thomas Berry 1770–Before 1812
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 8: Elizabeth Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 10: David J. Berry →

Ancestor Profile: Thomas Berry — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 9#

Thomas Berry 1770 – Before 1812#

It is almost impossible to write about Thomas Berry without including younger brother, William Berry, at the same time. The two brothers were involved in so many of life’s endeavors together. The William and Elizabeth Smith Cate family is a central point in both of their lives. Thomas and William Berry married two of their three daughters. This is a case of two brothers who married two sisters. This was not at all uncommon in the early days of our nation. William Berry married Hannah Cate first and Thomas married Sarah Cate a year later: It gets a lot more complicated after that.

item.title: Hannah Cate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Hannah Cate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 3 (by marriage) Hannah Cate Berry

Born
1770, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
1858, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
William Berry — married August 12, 1799, Orange County, NC
Parents
William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Hannah Cate Berry#

Hannah Cate was born in 1770 and she died in 1858 in Orange County, NC (1810 and 1850 Orange County Censuses; William Cate’s will, dated November 10, 1775, confirms children). Hannah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate.

item.title: Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Thomas Berry × Sarah Cate — Orange County, NC → Wayne County, TN

Generation 3 (by marriage) Sarah Cate Berry Pigg

Born
December 27, 1782, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
March 7, 1875, Wayne County, Tennessee
Married
1st: Thomas Berry — married August 11, 1800, Orange County, NC · 2nd: John Pigg — married December 11, 1815, Williamson County, TN
Parents
William Cate and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Sarah Cate#

Sarah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate and was born in North Carolina on December 27, 1782. Nothing is known about Sarah’s early life except that she had two brothers and two sisters. The brothers were John Cate and Benjamin Cate and her two sisters were Charity and Hannah Cate.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. William Clarence Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4William Clarence Berry × Sally Bowles / Massa Ann Tompkins — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL → Burnet County, TX

Generation 4 William Clarence Berry

Born
October 17, 1803, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
September 8, 1878, Burnet County, Texas
Married
1st: Sally Bowles (married November 20, 1826; died 1833) — 2nd: Massa Ann Tompkins (married 1836)
Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

William Clarence Berry, son of William and Hannah Cate Berry#

William Clarence Berry was born on October 17th 1803 in Orange County, North Carolina and died on 08 September 1878 in Burnet County Texas. He and his two brothers were listed on Hannah Cate Berry’s 1810 census. The family was living in the old Patrick Rutherford plantation house which Robert Berry bought in 1787. They may have lived there for many years but Hannah did not list in the 1820 Orange County, NC, census.

item.title: Berry Marriage Bonds
item.description:
Berry Marriage Bonds

Marriage bond records from the Grooms Book of Berry Marriages, now integrated into the Family Timeline.

The marriage bond data that was previously on this page has been migrated to the Family Timeline. All 39 Berry marriage bonds from the Grooms Book are now searchable and filterable using the “marriage” category filter on the timeline.

item.title: Elizabeth Berry Franklin
item.description:
Elizabeth Berry Franklin, daughter of John Berry and Hannah Cate#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 4 Elizabeth Berry × Theodrick Franklin — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, Alabama

Elizabeth Berry was born in Orange County NC in 1812. Hannah Cate Berry and John Berry were Elizabeth’s parents Elizabeth had three half brothers, William Clarence Berry, Robert C. Berry, and Thomas Person Berry. Temperance Jane Franklin was born in Orange County North Carolina in 1831.

item.title: Robert Berry, Son of David J. and Mary Blalock
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (David J. line)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Eliza Everett — Orange County, NC → Fayette/Cobb County, Georgia

Generation 4 Robert Berry

Born
August 2, 1812, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
Georgia
Married
Eliza Everett (married June 16, 1833)
Parents
David J. Berry and Mary Blalock
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

ROBERT BERRY was born on August 2, 1812 in Orange County North Carolina was named for his grandfather Robert Berry who owned the Fiddleton Plantation. He was about sixteen years old when his father David J. Berry decided to remove the family to Georgia. Robert had an Uncle and some Berry Cousins in Fayette County Alabama, Wayne County Tennessee, and some Camp cousins in Georgia. David and his family apparently chose to settle near his sister, Mary Berry Camp’s children, in Fayette County Georgia.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
William Clarence Berry (1803–1877)

Son of William and Hannah Cate Berry, from Orange County NC to Fayette County Alabama and Burnet County Texas.

William Clarence Berry, son of William and Hannah Cate Berry Berry#

ANCESTORS

Generation 1 Robert and Mary Williamson Berry VA
